turns.text: Q3 was another strong quarter. We added 5.07 million paid subscribers to reach 282.7 million. Revenue was $9.83 billion, up 15% year-over-year, at the top of our guidance range. Operating margin was 30%, significantly above our 28% guidance. We are now comfortable saying we will reach 300 million subscribers by end of Q4 2024. Live events are performing better than expected — the Jake Paul vs Mike Tyson boxing event in November will be the largest live streaming event in history.

Operating income of $2.9 billion represents a 30% operating margin, well above guidance. For Q4, we expect revenue of $10.1 billion and an operating margin of 22%, reflecting content investments. Full year 2024 operating margin remains guided to 26%. We expect 2025 revenue growth of 11 to 13% and an operating margin of 28 to 29%.
Our next question comes from Mark Mahaney at Evercore ISI.
Spencer, how should we think about the trajectory of advertising revenue as a percentage of total revenue, and when does it become a meaningful contributor?

statements.claim: Q3 2024 revenue was $9.83 billion with a 30% operating margin, above guidance of $9.825B and 28%.
Netflix will reach 300 million subscribers by end of Q4 2024.
2025 revenue will grow 11 to 13% with an operating margin of 28 to 29%.
The Jake Paul vs Mike Tyson boxing event will be the largest live streaming event in history.
Q4 2024 revenue guidance is $10.1 billion with an operating margin of 22%.
